title: podman login doesn't accept -u -p flags
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.attempt to login with -u and -p flags returns "Error: too many arguments, login takes only 1 argument"

2.login without -u and -p results in podman prompting for user and password - this works

**Describe the results you received:**

I'm running the fedoracoreos AMI 

fedora-coreos-31.20200310.3.0-hvm (ami-0c0261fbc08b1b65a)

I've granted the core user access to the docker socket

sudo chmod 666 /var/run/docker.sock

I'm using podman to login to an AWS ECR container repository. This EC2 instance has an IAM role with a policy granting access to this repo. Since login works when podman is run in user/pw prompt mode there is nothing wrong with this config.

The only thing wrong is that podman doesn't support its -u and -p flags.

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expect login using -u and -p to work.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.8.1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.6
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:

host:

  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "31"
  IDMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  MemFree: 316825600
  MemTotal: 1019379712
  OCIRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: runc-1.0.0-102.dev.gitdc9208a.fc31.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc10
      commit: 96f6022b37cbe12b26c9ad33a24677bec72a9cc3
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 1
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: ip-10-0-6-155
  kernel: 5.5.8-200.fc31.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    Executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    Package: slirp4netns-0.4.0-20.1.dev.gitbbd6f25.fc31.x86_64
    Version: |-
      slirp4netns version 0.4.0-beta.3+dev
      commit: bbd6f25c70d5db2a1cd3bfb0416a8db99a75ed7e
  uptime: 1h 23m 25.13s (Approximately 0.04 days)

  search:

        FUSE library version 3.6.2
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.29
```

comments.body:
I failed to replicate the issue. can you provide more info on the podman command you are using?

```
podman login -u testname -p testpassword docker.io
WARNING! Using --password via the cli is insecure. Please consider using --password-stdin
Login Succeeded!
```

Here's an example that failed:

[core@ip-10-0-6-155 ~]$ podman login –u AWS –p
""
778349714046.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
Error: too many arguments, login takes only 1 argument


Here's an example that succeded where the user/pw were entered via login
prompts:

[core@ip-10-0-6-155 ~]$ podman login
778349714046.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
Username: AWS
Password:
Login Succeeded!

On Thu, Apr 9, 2020 at 7:15 AM Qi Wang <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> I failed to replicate the issue. can you provide more info on the podman
> command you are using?
>
> podman login -u testname -p testpassword docker.io
> WARNING! Using --password via the cli is insecure. Please consider using --password-stdin
> Login Succeeded!
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you authored the thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/5772#issuecomment-611551153>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AAGJFB44H4EL6MVJ42TGZKTRLXKABANCNFSM4MERFRXQ>
> .
>
-p requires a PASSWORDSTRING and we require you to enter the registry.  We don't hard code docker.io.
@hapnermw  `–u AWS –p ...` your dash `–` looks not correct, should be `-`

I don't understand your note. Exactly what do you think is wrong with my
failed example?

On Thu, Apr 9, 2020 at 11:23 AM Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> -p requires a PASSWORDSTRING and we require you to enter the registry. We
> don't hard code docker.io.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you authored the thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/5772#issuecomment-611681438>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AAGJFB6C2QRHSEZCDCQ7CYLRLYHDLANCNFSM4MERFRXQ>
> .
>

@hapnermw it will work if you change the `–u –p` to `-u -p`


podman login -u AWS -p "" 778349714046.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
WARNING! Using --password via the cli is insecure. Please consider using --password-stdin
Login Succeeded!

You are right. That is strange. I likely copied the original command from
an example that had the wrong dash character. I'll try to find it.

On Thu, Apr 9, 2020 at 11:30 AM Qi Wang <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> @hapnermw <https://github.com/hapnermw> –u AWS –p ... your dash – looks
> not correct, should be -
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/5772#issuecomment-611684575>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AAGJFB4G7C4VFX4PWI7NQR3RLYH37ANCNFSM4MERFRXQ>
> .
>

Thanks for catching that. You can close the issue.

Sorry to have taken your time with such a stupid; but, hard to see problem.

Somewhere there is an example login with this issue ...

On Thu, Apr 9, 2020 at 11:39 AM Qi Wang <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> @hapnermw <https://github.com/hapnermw> it will work if you change the –u
> –p to -u -p
>
> podman login -u AWS -p
> ""
> 778349714046.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
> WARNING! Using --password via the cli is insecure. Please consider using
> --password-stdin
> Login Succeeded!

@hapnermw If you do track down that bad example, please do let us know or better yet, open up a PR.
